bits.premise: A vasectomy question is misdirected: the prerequisite is not readiness for fatherhood but having sex at all.
The Kentucky incest stereotype is subverted: the cousin was unattainable, and the family had already claimed her.
A religious denomination name is used as a pun to announce a meth addiction.

lines.text: My big brother recently told me he got a vasectomy,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: and then he asked me if I was gonna get a vasectomy.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: I was like, "I think I gotta be getting some pussy first."
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: sometimes people ask me if I banged my cousin.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: She was way out of my league.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: Plus, I had a big brother and like six cousins.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: My family had some real stiff competition.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: Growing up in Kentucky, sometimes people think I was brought up Baptists, but I was actually
lines.label: setup

lines.text: And in my 20s I became a Crystal Methodist.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: Thank you everybody, I'm Kyle Clinton.
lines.label: fluff
